page_content: INST725 Legal Research for Information Professionals (3 Credits) An in-depth exploration the methods, resources, and context of conducting advanced legal research. After offering an overview of various types of legal materials, the course will focus on ﬁnding and analyzing legal materials through various primary sources, databases, secondary sources, and public records for government and corporate settings. The course will also discuss practical issues of conducting legal research, such as data management and budgeting. Prerequisite: INST615. Restriction: Permission of INFO-College of Information Studies.
